# Rust SDK

Rust SDK (`privy-rs`) for the Privy wallet API. Uses Progenitor for code generation from an OpenAPI spec.

## Generated Files — DO NOT MODIFY

- `crates/privy-openapi/` — fully generated by `cargo-progenitor` from `openapi.json`
- `$OUT_DIR/subclients.rs` — generated at build time by `build.rs` from `allowlist.yml`

To regenerate: `mise gen-openapi`

## Architecture

```
crates/privy-openapi/     Generated types + base HTTP client (Progenitor)
build.rs + allowlist.yml  Generates subclient wrappers (WalletsClient, UsersClient, etc.)
src/subclients/           Hand-written extensions (rpc, raw_sign, update, export, import)
src/ethereum.rs           High-level Ethereum convenience methods
src/solana.rs             High-level Solana convenience methods
src/keys.rs              AuthorizationContext + P-256 signing
src/utils.rs             Request signing/formatting utilities
src/privy_hpke.rs        HPKE encryption for wallet export
src/client.rs            PrivyClient initialization and configuration
```

## Adding New Behavior

1. If the method needs authorization signing, add a wrapper in `src/subclients/` that calls `generate_authorization_signatures()` then delegates to the generated `_method`.
2. For new resources/endpoints, update `allowlist.yml` and rebuild.
3. For high-level chain operations, extend `src/ethereum.rs` or `src/solana.rs`.

## Commands

```sh
cargo build              # Build (triggers build.rs code generation)
cargo test               # Run all tests (needs PRIVY_TEST_* env vars)
cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings
mise gen-openapi         # Pull latest spec + regenerate (requires nightly)
mise pull-openapi        # Pull + patch spec only (no regeneration)
mise gen-p256-key        # Generate a test P-256 key pair
```
